question_text: Two levers of different lengths are used to lift the same load. The shorter lever requires a larger force. Explain why, using the concept of moments.
correct_answer: To lift the same load, the moment must be the same. Since moment = force × distance, if the distance is smaller on the shorter lever, the force must be larger to produce the same moment.
explanation: A lever works by balancing moments around a pivot. Moment is calculated by multiplying force by the perpendicular distance from the pivot. For a lever to lift a load, the turning effect (moment) on both sides of the pivot must be equal. When using a shorter lever, the distance from the pivot is smaller, so a larger force must be applied to create the same moment and lift the load.
